message: agent1: working on extracting normalize_image_bytes helper in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py (image_to_base64 refactor). Will touch that file only.

agent2: implementing get_image_mimetype() lazy accessor on ImageBlock in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py. Will refactor image_to_base64 to use it. Let me know if you're touching this file too.

agent1: heads up, I'm also editing image_to_base64 in that same file — added a normalize_image_bytes(image) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) helper function right above ImageBlock, and image_to_base64 now calls it. Already tested, all existing tests pass. Can you build get_image_mimetype() as a separate concern that composes with/calls normalize_image_bytes for the decode step, so we don't stomp each other? Let me know how you want to merge — happy to adjust to fit your accessor if you show me the shape.

agent2: I edited image_to_base64 in types.py — it now does: self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image); self.get_image_mimetype(); return self. New method get_image_mimetype() decodes self.image (already base64) via base64.b64decode and guesses mimetype with filetype.guess, caching to self.image_mimetype. If your normalize_image_bytes helper also touches image_to_base64, please rebase on top of this so we don't clobber each other. Happy to adjust if you already have your version applied.

agent1: FYI we appear to be in separate sandboxes (my copy of types.py doesn't show your get_image_mimetype edit). My normalize_image_bytes helper + image_to_base64 refactor is finished and tests pass on my end — finalizing my patch now. Good luck with yours.

agent2: Interesting — in my sandbox, git diff only shows my own edit to types.py, not yours, so we appear to be working in isolated copies (not a shared filesystem) despite both targeting llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py. My final shape: image_to_base64 now does 'self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image); self.get_image_mimetype(); return self', and get_image_mimetype() does 'if not self.image_mimetype and self.image: guess = filetype.guess(base64.b64decode(self.image)); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None; return self.image_mimetype'. Since our sandboxes seem isolated, I'll finalize my own patch as-is — feel free to adapt yours to match this shape if it helps your evaluation, but no actual file conflict on my end.
